rulings.Case Title: AMY MORAN VS. QWEST COMMUNICATIONS et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 29, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANTS QWEST COMMUNICATIONS, DENNIS SHERWOOD MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Motion for summary judgment denied. Motion for summary adjudication granted in part and denied in part. Summary adjudication granted for first cause of action, discrimination. No evidence that adverse action was taken because of plaintiff's gender. Defendant offers a nondiscriminatory reason for the conduct (to bring the quota in line with branch expectations) and plaintiff fails to establish this is a mere pretext. Motion denied for second cause of action, harassment. Triable issue of fact whether conduct severe and pervasive. (Moran declaration.) Triable issue of fact whether Sherwood's conduct was such to create a hostile working environment. Motion denied for third cause of action, failure to prevent harassmsent. Triable issue of fact raised whether defendant's procedure sufficient to address plaintiff's harassmsent concerns. Motion denied for the fourth cause of action, termination in violation of public policy. Triable issue of fact raised whether working conditions intolerable due to the treatment she received from Sherwood and Qwest's disregard of her complaint. Summary adjudication granted as to fifth cause of action for failure to make immediate payment of wages upon discharge and waiting time penalties. No triable issue of fact that plaintiff was paid all the compensation she was entitled to under the compensation agreement. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 29, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Off Calendar, without prejudice in view of court's ruling on demurrer. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PHILIP THOMASCHIMA VS. ESURANCE INCORPORATED, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Deft Demurrer To Pltf'S First Amended Complaint; Request For Judicial Notice In Support Of Demurrer To First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, December 29, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT ESURANCE, INCORPORATED Demurrer To Plaintiff's First Amended Complaint. Demurrer as to the first, second, third and fourth causes of action sustained with ten days leave to amend. As to the first and second causes of action, plaintiff must plead with specificity how plaintiff has met the requirements of Insurance Code section 11580.2(i). Insufficient facts regarding bodily injury suit filed aginst uninsured motorist or waiver or estoppel. The third cause of action pleads facts that essentially amount to bad faith claims that are subject to Insurance Code section 790.03. Plaintiff may not plead around the absolute bar to relief created by that statute by recasting the cause of action as one for unfair competition. Plaintiff to plead fourth cause of action for fraud with specificity: who, what, where, when, and why. =(302/CWW)
